disease.name: Blood Culture Interpretation
disease.note: Interpreting blood culture results to distinguish true bacteremia from contamination. Timing, number of sets, and organisms matter.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: True pathogen vs contaminant determines clinical significance

subtypes.name: True Bacteremia
subtypes.management: Targeted antibiotics based on sensitivity. Source control. Repeat cultures to document clearance. Monitor for complications (endocarditis, septic metastases).

subtypes.name: Likely Contaminant
subtypes.management: No treatment if truly contaminant. Repeat cultures if suspicion remains. Common contaminants: Coagulase-negative Staph, Corynebacterium, Bacillus, Propionibacterium.

laboratory.name: Blood culture sets (2 sets, 2 bottles each)

pathways.criteria: typical pathogen, >=2 positive bottles, clinical correlation
pathways.treatment: Targeted antibiotics de-escalated from empiric. Repeat cultures at 48-72h. Duration 7-14 days depending on source and organism.

pathways.criteria: single positive, skin flora, unclear clinical relevance
pathways.treatment: Repeat blood cultures. Review if still clinically indicated. If growth confirmed, treat. If no growth and well, consider contaminant.
